perturbation.original_text: The Base Consulting Fee shall be payable in accordance with the Company's normal payroll procedures in effect from time to time. All subsequent monthly payments of Base Consulting Fee shall be paid within the first five days of the following calendar month.
perturbation.changed_text: The Base Consulting Fee shall be payable in accordance with the Company's normal payroll procedures in effect from time to time. All subsequent monthly payments of Base Consulting Fee shall be paid within the last five days of the current calendar month.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadline for the Consultant's Base Consulting Fee. Originally, payments were due within the first five days of the following month. The modification shifts the deadline to within the last five days of the current month. This contradicts the explicit payment timing stated earlier, creating uncertainty and potential disputes regarding when the Consultant should expect payment. This change creates an inconsistency on when to pay for the fees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Remuneration. (a) The Consultant's monthly base consulting fee shall be three thousand two hundred fifty United States dollars ($3,250 (together with any increases thereto as hereinafter provided, the "Base Consulting Fee") through 19 August 2020. Commencing on 20 August 2020, Consultant's Base Consulting Fee shall be increased to $8,500 per calendar month. The Base Consulting Fee shall be payable in accordance with the Company's normal payroll procedures in effect from time to time. All subsequent monthly payments of Base Consulting Fee shall be paid within the last five days of the current calendar month. The Base Consulting Fee may be increased by the Board from time to time during the Term, but shall be reviewed by the Board at least annually (b) The Company shall also issue Consultant as soon as reasonably practicable warrants to purchase 1,250,000 shares of common stock in the form attached hereto as Annex A. The Company agrees to bear all costs and fees to be charged by the Company's transfer agent in respect of such shares. (c) The Consultant shall be eligible to participate in benefit plans currently and hereafter maintained by the Company of general applicability to other consultants of the Company. Subject to the following sentence, the Consultant will be entitled to receive up to a one-time bonus of 1,500,000 shares of common stock of the Company promptly after the value of the Company's outstanding stock equals $100 million dollars. (d) In addition to the foregoing, the Company will grant the Consultant additional compensation in the form of cash or shares in cases of extraordinary contribution by him to the benefit of the Company as the Board of Directors of the Company will decide.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadline, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which now includes the altered payment deadline. The original text specified payment within the first five days of the *following* month, while the changed text mandates payment within the last five days of the *current* month. This creates a clear inconsistency and confusion regarding when the consultant should be paid, directly contradicting the payment terms outlined in the contradicted text (2.1(a)).

perturbation.original_text: The Company may terminate the Consultant's engagement for Cause.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company must terminate the Consultant's engagement for Cause.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the Company's obligation regarding termination for Cause. Originally, the Company had the option to terminate the Consultant's engagement for Cause. The modification changes this to a mandatory obligation. This contradicts with the following section that states the 30 day notice, since now the text implies that company is obligated to termiante for cause. Now it is mandatory and not optional anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Termination of Engagement. The Consultant's engagement may be terminated only as follows: (a) Termination by the Company (i) For Cause. The Company must terminate the Consultant's engagement for Cause. (ii) Without Cause. The Company may terminate Consultant's engagement at any time by giving Consultant 60 days prior written Notice of the termination. In such case, 100% of the Consultant's unvested stock and, if applicable, option or warrant compensation of any nature will vest without any further action required on the part of the Consultant or the Company and the Company will deliver to the order of the Consultant promptly upon receipt of a written demand of the Consultant such shares of common stock or options at its sole expense as become due to Consultant hereunder. The Consultant's right to receive compensation whether in cash or securities shall survive any termination of this Agreement Without Cause. (b) Termination by the Consultant (i) For Good Reason. The Consultant may terminate the Consultant's engagement with the Company for Good Reason. (ii) Without Good Reason. The Consultant may voluntarily terminate the Consultant's engagement with the Company at any time by giving the Company 120 days prior written Notice of the termination. (c) Termination Upon Death or Disability (i) Death. The Consultant's engagement shall terminate upon the Consultant's death. (ii) Disability. The Company may terminate the Consultant's engagement upon the Consultant's Disability.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Company *may* terminate for cause, implying an option. The changed text states that the Company *must* terminate for cause, making it an obligation. This directly contradicts the later section of the contract (4.1 (a)(ii)), which provides for termination *without* cause, including a 60-day notice period and vesting of stock options. If the Company *must* terminate for cause, there is no possibility of choosing to terminate without cause. This alteration removes the company's option to terminate without cause as described later on in the document, causing an in-text contradiction, because the original gave the company an option which is now removed.

perturbation.original_text: This Warrant is exercisable in whole or in part at any time and from time to time on or before the earlier of 5:00 pm GMT on the tenth (10th) anniversary of the Issue Date.
perturbation.changed_text: This Warrant is exercisable in whole or in part at any time and from time to time on or before the earlier of 5:00 pm EST on the fifth (5th) anniversary of the Issue Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the warrant's expiration date and time zone. Initially, the warrant was exercisable until 5:00 pm GMT on the tenth anniversary of the Issue Date. The alteration moves the deadline to 5:00 pm EST on the fifth anniversary. This directly contradicts the original expiration timeline. This will affect the value of the warrant depending on the appreciation of the stocks as the expiry date of the warrant have been reduced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Term. This Warrant is exercisable in whole or in part at any time and from time to time on or before the earlier of 5:00 pm EST on the fifth (5th) anniversary of the Issue Date. 4.2 Legends. This Warrant and the Shares (and the securities issuable, directly or indirectly, upon conversion of the Shares, if any) shall be imprinted with a legend in substantially the following form: THIS WARRANT AND THE SHARES ISSUABLE HEREUNDER HAVE NOT BEEN REGISTERED UNDER THE SECURITIES ACT OF 1933, AS AMENDED (THE "ACT"), OR THE SECURITIES LAWS OF ANY STATE AND MAY NOT BE OFFERED, SOLD OR OTHERWISE TRANSFERRED, PLEDGED OR HYPOTHECATED UNLESS AND UNTIL REGISTERED UNDER SAID ACT AND APPLICABLE STATE SECURITIES LAW OR, IN THE OPINION OF LEGAL COUNSEL IN FORM AND SUBSTANCE SATISFACTORY TO THE ISSUER OF THESE SECURITIES, SUCH OFFER, SALE OR TRANSFER, PLEDGE OR HYPOTHECATION IS EXEMPT FROM REGISTRATION.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the warrant's expiration date and time zone from 5:00 pm GMT on the tenth anniversary to 5:00 pm EST on the fifth anniversary. The contradicted text states 'This Warrant is exercisable in whole or in part at any time and from time to time on or before the earlier of 5:00 pm EST on the fifth (5th) anniversary of the Issue Date.', and the changed text now conflicts with this fixed expiration timeline.
